title: initiate-db
text:
This script outlines the process to automate the updating of a duckdb database for new file released by NEON. This process uses the neonstore package to manage data file download and storage for NEON data releases. The database of up-to-date files are stored within the data/database-files/ folder. The updating of data files will be automated approximately every 6 months through github actions. This will allow locally running scripts to convert the data into usable formats for size spectra analysis.
To establish the initial database neonstore was used to download stream macroinvertebrate and fish data products. We first set the local database directory (e.g., C:/Users/jj0895/OneDrive - UNT System/Projects/Size-Spectra-Boogie/NEONSizeData/data/database-files) and pass this directly to the neon_download() function.

library(neonstore)
library(arrow)

Using the neon_download() function we can automate the download into a pre-existing folder using established credentials. In the automated script, this is handled with the env variable ${{ github.workspace}}/data/database-files. The data products for download are controlled with the dps object. Then they are added to the database with neon_store()

streamsites=c("HOPB", "LEWI", "POSE", "CUPE",
              "GUIL", "KING", "MCDI", "LECO",
              "WALK", "MAYF", "ARIK", "BLUE",
              "PRIN", "BLDE", "COMO", "WLOU",
              "SYCA", "REDB", "MART", "MCRA",
              "BIGC", "TECR", "OKSR", "CARI")

db_path = here::here('data/database-files/')

dps = c("DP1.20120.001","DP1.20107.001","DP1.20190.001")

neon_download(product = dps,
              dir = db_path)

neon_store(dir = here::here('data/database-files/'),
           db = neon_db(dir = db_path, read_only = FALSE))

Once these initial files are downloaded and added to the database, these objects are uploaded to the bucket. This is accomplished by first exporting the database as parquet files to a temporary directory:

# create a temporary directory for the export parquet files
export_dir = file.path(tempdir(), 'parquet')

# export the local parquet file structure to the local database
neon_export_db(dir = export_dir,
               db = neon_db(dir = here::here('data/database-files/')))

# we can check the files in the temporary directory for uploading
list.files(export_dir,
           recursive = TRUE,
           full.names = TRUE)

We then set the specifications of the s3 bucket. This bucket is publicly readable:

# set the s3 bucket specs 
bucket = "neonsizedata-392202703749-us-east-2-an"
prefix = "neonstore/parquet"


neon_sync_db(s3 = s3,
             dir = export_dir)

We can view the files available on the s3 bucket using the aws.s3 package to view the contents.

library(aws.s3)
get_bucket(bucket = bucket,
           prefix = prefix)

And we can connect to the s3 bucket to perform actions locally by connecting to the remote bucket:

s3 = arrow::s3_bucket(bucket = paste0(bucket,"/",prefix,"/"))

remote = neon_remote_db(bucket = s3)

and then reading in a file from the remote connection.

neon_remote(
  table = "inv_taxonomyProcessed-basic", 
  product = "DP1.20120.001",
  db = remote
  )  %>%
  dplyr::collect() %>% 
  dplyr::glimpse()

Columns: 52

This script outlines the process to automate the updating of a duckdb database for new file released by NEON. This process uses the neonstore package to manage data file download and storage for NEON data releases. The database of up-to-date files are stored within the data/database-files/ folder. The updating of data files will be automated approximately every 6 months through github actions. This will allow locally running scripts to convert the data into usable formats for size spectra analysis.
To establish the initial database neonstore was used to download stream macroinvertebrate and fish data products. We first set the local database directory (e.g., C:/Users/jj0895/OneDrive - UNT System/Projects/Size-Spectra-Boogie/NEONSizeData/data/database-files) and pass this directly to the neon_download() function.

library(neonstore)
library(arrow)

Using the neon_download() function we can automate the download into a pre-existing folder using established credentials. In the automated script, this is handled with the env variable ${{ github.workspace}}/data/database-files. The data products for download are controlled with the dps object. Then they are added to the database with neon_store()

streamsites=c("HOPB", "LEWI", "POSE", "CUPE",
              "GUIL", "KING", "MCDI", "LECO",
              "WALK", "MAYF", "ARIK", "BLUE",
              "PRIN", "BLDE", "COMO", "WLOU",
              "SYCA", "REDB", "MART", "MCRA",
              "BIGC", "TECR", "OKSR", "CARI")

db_path = here::here('data/database-files/')

dps = c("DP1.20120.001","DP1.20107.001","DP1.20190.001")

neon_download(product = dps,
              dir = db_path)

neon_store(dir = here::here('data/database-files/'),
           db = neon_db(dir = db_path, read_only = FALSE))

Once these initial files are downloaded and added to the database, these objects are uploaded to the bucket. This is accomplished by first exporting the database as parquet files to a temporary directory:

# create a temporary directory for the export parquet files
export_dir = file.path(tempdir(), 'parquet')

# export the local parquet file structure to the local database
neon_export_db(dir = export_dir,
               db = neon_db(dir = here::here('data/database-files/')))

# we can check the files in the temporary directory for uploading
list.files(export_dir,
           recursive = TRUE,
           full.names = TRUE)

We then set the specifications of the s3 bucket. This bucket is publicly readable:

# set the s3 bucket specs 
bucket = "neonsizedata-392202703749-us-east-2-an"
prefix = "neonstore/parquet"


neon_sync_db(s3 = s3,
             dir = export_dir)

We can view the files available on the s3 bucket using the aws.s3 package to view the contents.

library(aws.s3)
get_bucket(bucket = bucket,
           prefix = prefix)

And we can connect to the s3 bucket to perform actions locally by connecting to the remote bucket:

s3 = arrow::s3_bucket(bucket = paste0(bucket,"/",prefix,"/"))

remote = neon_remote_db(bucket = s3)

and then reading in a file from the remote connection.

neon_remote(
  table = "inv_taxonomyProcessed-basic", 
  product = "DP1.20120.001",
  db = remote
  )  %>%
  dplyr::collect() %>% 
  dplyr::glimpse()
